title: distictis lactiflora
intro: Distictis lactiflora, also known as the white milk-flowers or white coral root, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Bignoniaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The health benefits of Distictis lactiflora include its ability to relieve respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate menstrual cramps. Herbal preparations of Distictis lactiflora include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
